message:
fix: clean up lock directories in parallel sync and global cleanup

Fixed two bugs where lock directories were not being cleaned up:

1. cleanup() function now removes RESULTS_LOCK_DIR in addition to
   RESULTS_FILE. This prevents stale lock directories from accumulating
   in the state directory.

2. run_parallel_sync() now explicitly removes the queue, results, and
   progress lock directories after workers finish. Previously only the
   temp files were removed, leaving empty lock directories behind if
   workers didn't release them cleanly.

Also added cleanup of ${work_queue}.tmp which is created during queue
manipulation.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2324,8 +2324,9 @@ run_parallel_sync() {
         esac
     done < "$results_file"
 
-    # Cleanup temp files
-    rm -f "$work_queue" "$results_file" "$lock_base" "$progress_file" 2>/dev/null
+    # Cleanup temp files and lock directories
+    rm -f "$work_queue" "${work_queue}.tmp" "$results_file" "$lock_base" "$progress_file" 2>/dev/null
+    rmdir "$queue_lock_dir" "$results_lock_dir" "$progress_lock_dir" 2>/dev/null || true
 
     # Return results via global variables (for summary)
     PARALLEL_CLONED=$cloned
@@ -2348,10 +2349,13 @@ cleanup() {
         return
     fi
 
-    # Remove temp files
+    # Remove temp files and lock directories
     if [[ -n "${RESULTS_FILE:-}" && -f "$RESULTS_FILE" ]]; then
         rm -f "$RESULTS_FILE"
     fi
+    if [[ -n "${RESULTS_LOCK_DIR:-}" && -d "$RESULTS_LOCK_DIR" ]]; then
+        rmdir "$RESULTS_LOCK_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true
+    fi
 }
 
 # Set up trap for cleanup
